Value: カルシウムの積極的な服用による腎臓結石予防法・活性吸収型海草カルシウム (AAACa) の尿中蓚酸排泄抑制作用機序
日本老年医学会雑誌 (Japanese Journal of Geriatrics)
No specific restrictions or regulations regarding lifestyle, exercise, or diet

Data Field: Loss to Follow-up (n per arm if reported)
Baseline Stone Size or Burden (if reported)
Outcome A: Stone Recurrence / New Stone Formation (DICHOTOMOUS)

Value: Calcium oxalate crystals in urine (microscopic exam)
Next morning first urine (average of 3 days)

Data Field: Withdrawals Due to Adverse Events (n per arm)
Data Requires Imputation for SD? (Yes / No / Not sure)

Value: The study utilized 9 healthy volunteers (4 males, 5 females) aged 23-49 in a crossover design comparing AAACa, CaCO3, and Placebo. Results showed AAACa tended to decrease urinary oxalate and calcium-oxalate products more efficiently than CaCO3.
